div.head: The Ambassador in the United Kingdom (Kennedy) to the Secretary of State
div.p: 1396. From Rublee. Your 732, November 30, 7 p.m. We immediately urged upon the German intermediary, who is arranging the meeting at Brussels on Thursday,December 8. the possibility of having a British financial expert accompany Pell. He has now replied that his principals would rather call off the meeting than have a British representative present. They are prepared to make a business proposition to Pell as Assistant Director of the Committee and are prepared to have CottonJoseph P. Cotton, Jr., assistant to the Director of the Intergovernmental Committee. present as my personal representative.
I might add that Cotton is fully qualified to report on financial matters. [Rublee.]
